file_insights.changes: Added new npm script 'verify:e2e' that runs the E2E flow verification script using tsx

Created comprehensive E2E verification script (531 lines) covering full user workflow: registration, login, organization creation, menu creation, menu publishing, and order placement with status transitions
Created Vitest E2E test suite with comprehensive test cases for the entire application flow
Implemented reusable API client helper for making authenticated requests in E2E tests
Created barrel export for E2E test helpers
Implemented test data factory for generating consistent E2E test data with unique identifiers

patterns_discovered.description: Created standalone verification script and matching Vitest test suite to validate complete user journey from registration through order completion
patterns_discovered.usage: Enables continuous verification of critical application workflows without manual testing

patterns_discovered.description: Implemented reusable API helper with support for authentication tokens, organization headers, and consistent response parsing
patterns_discovered.usage: Centralizes HTTP request logic for E2E tests and scripts, reducing code duplication

patterns_discovered.description: Tests organized into logical phases (Authentication, Organization, Menu, Publishing, Order Placement, Status Workflow) with clear dependencies
patterns_discovered.usage: Makes test flow easy to follow and understand, helps identify where failures occur in the workflow

patterns_discovered.description: Terminal output color-coded with semantic meanings (green for success, red for errors, cyan for info, yellow for subheaders)
patterns_discovered.usage: Improves readability and debugging experience when running E2E verification scripts

patterns_discovered.description: Tracks total steps, successes, and failures during verification with final summary report
patterns_discovered.usage: Provides quick health check of entire workflow, identifies failure rates at a glance

gotchas_discovered.description: Multiple levels of nested type casting required for extracting data from API responses due to generic Record<string, unknown> types
gotchas_discovered.impact: Code readability reduced, prone to runtime errors if response structure changes
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Could benefit from stronger typing with generated TypeScript types from API schema

gotchas_discovered.description: Uses Date.now() for unique identifiers which could theoretically create collisions within the same millisecond across parallel test runs
gotchas_discovered.impact: Unlikely but possible test interference in high-concurrency scenarios
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Could add random suffix or use UUID for guaranteed uniqueness

gotchas_discovered.description: Script assumes application is running at http://localhost:3000 with no validation that endpoint is actually available
gotchas_discovered.impact: Cryptic errors if server is not running, no early failure detection
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Could add health check endpoint call at script start to verify connectivity

gotchas_discovered.description: Assumes all status transitions (confirm → prepare → ready → deliver → complete) are valid sequential operations without validating business rules
gotchas_discovered.impact: Tests may pass with incorrect state machine implementation if transitions are allowed out of sequence
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add validation that status transitions follow expected business rules

gotchas_discovered.description: Content-type header check for JSON parsing could fail silently, leaving body as empty object
gotchas_discovered.impact: Difficult to debug API responses that aren't JSON, error messages would be unclear
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add explicit error handling and logging for non-JSON responses

approach_outcome.description: Successfully implemented comprehensive end-to-end verification covering the complete critical user journey: user registration, authentication, organization creation, menu creation with categories and products, menu publishing with QR code generation, order placement, and full order status workflow transitions
key_achievements: Created dual verification approach: standalone CLI script and Vitest test suite for flexibility
Implemented reusable test helpers and API client for maintainability
Generated unique test data per run to allow repeated testing without conflicts
Included colored console output and step tracking for excellent developer experience
Covered all major application features in a single cohesive workflow
Validated both happy-path and data persistence through full workflow

quality_metrics.code_organization: Excellent - clear phase separation and helper structure
quality_metrics.maintainability: Good - reusable helpers and clear test data factory
quality_metrics.developer_experience: Good - colored output, clear logging, easy to understand flow

recommendations.recommendation: Add health check endpoint call at script startup to verify API connectivity before running tests
recommendations.rationale: Fails fast with clear error message rather than cryptic response parsing errors

recommendations.recommendation: Generate TypeScript types from API schema to replace nested Record<string, unknown> casting
recommendations.rationale: Improves code readability, catches response structure changes at compile time

recommendations.recommendation: Replace Date.now() with UUID v4 for test data generation to guarantee uniqueness in parallel scenarios
recommendations.rationale: Prevents theoretical race conditions in concurrent test execution

recommendations.recommendation: Add business rule validation for order status transitions to catch invalid state machine implementations
recommendations.rationale: Tests should verify not just that transitions work, but that they follow business rules

recommendations.recommendation: Add retry logic with exponential backoff for flaky network requests
recommendations.rationale: Reduces false negatives from transient network failures in CI environments

recommendations.recommendation: Add JSDoc comments documenting expected response structures and API contract
recommendations.rationale: Makes test maintainability easier when API contracts change

recommendations.recommendation: Add execution time tracking per phase and export timing metrics for performance regression detection
recommendations.rationale: Enables tracking of performance regressions across commits
